behaviors.reasoning: Subdirectory traversal requires the watcher wired to the pipeline — testing the discovery→processing chain.
Dedup logic is testable with simulated duplicate requests — no real OS events needed, just the dedup module wired to the processor.
Real OS filesystem event behavior (multiple events per write) can only be tested with a real filesystem and real watcher — OS behavior varies by platform.
Directory removal + recreation is real OS behavior — inotify/FSEvents handle this differently per platform.
Timestamp ordering is testable with files wired through the pipeline — the sort logic works on metadata, not OS behavior.
Detecting incomplete writes requires real filesystem behavior — file locks, write-in-progress detection varies by OS.
